meaning: n.竞争者,对手v.竞争,对抗,相匹敌
examples: This year the newspaper has outsold its main rival.
Their smaller rival is battling to end their duopoly.
There is often no discernible difference between rival brands.
However, SEB appears to be weathering the troubles better than its rival, for now at least.
He fired back at McCain, accusing his rival of a little doublespeak of his own.
